pullRequest.title: Adds Keystroke Reflection Exfoliation
pullRequest.body:
Resolves #89
This pull request introduces exfiltration mode functionality, which leverages keyboard LED states to encode and extract data. The changes span across `boot.py`, `code.py`, and `duckyinpython.py`, adding detection, control, and monitoring logic for exfiltration mode, as well as asynchronous execution and new script commands. The most important changes are grouped below.

**Exfiltration Mode Implementation**

- Added detection of exfiltration mode by parsing the payload file for the `$_EXFIL_MODE_ENABLED` variable, and conditionally disabling the USB drive if exfiltration is enabled and no loot file exists (`boot.py`). This is because if the pico is visible and in HID mode it can not write files.
- Introduced an asynchronous `monitor_led_changes` task that monitors keyboard LED changes and writes encoded bits to `loot.bin` during exfiltration mode (`duckyinpython.py`).
- Added variables for exfiltration mode and LED control, and logic to force LED states during exfiltration (`duckyinpython.py`). When `$_EXFIL_LEDS_ENABLED` is true the light will be solid instead of blinking, and will resume blinking after the loot has been exfiltrated.

**Script Execution and Asynchronous Refactoring**

- Refactored script execution (`runScript` and `parseLine`) to be asynchronous, allowing payload execution and monitoring tasks to run concurrently (`code.py`, `duckyinpython.py`). This is to allow support for `WAIT_FOR_SCROLL_CHANGE`.
- Added logic to skip payload execution if `loot.bin` exists, preventing overwriting during exfiltration (`code.py`) as well as allowing user to see the loot.bin without execution

**Keyboard LED State Management**

- Implemented commands and functions to save and restore host keyboard lock states, and to wait for scroll lock changes. (`duckyinpython.py`).

**Expression and Variable Handling Improvements**

- Improved expression evaluation by normalizing Boolean values (`TRUE`/`FALSE`) and expanded the set of internal variables to support exfiltration features (`duckyinpython.py`).

**General Codebase Enhancements**

- Added necessary imports, cleaned up unused imports, and improved code structure to support new features and asynchronous patterns (`code.py`, `duckyinpython.py`).

**CAVEAT:**

Because of the issue with the while file writing, when you first plug in the Pico with the `$_EXFIL_MODE_ENABLED` set to true, it will not show up as a drive. Once the loot is saved, (uses scroll lock to signify end of data) the loot.bin file will be created, reinsert the Pico into the computer and it will now show up as a drive, allowing the user to examine the loot, the payload will also not run if a loot.bin exists.

pullRequest.title: Fixing asyncio issues in webapp
pullRequest.body:
Fixed incorrectly calling async functions from non-async code.
Fixed sleeps to be async aware
